card.intuition: In a distributed data store, it is impossible to simultaneously provide Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance.
card.analogy: A group project with 3 students: you can have it perfect (Consistency), turned in on time (Availability), or work separately when the internet goes out (Partition Tolerance). You can only pick 2.
when_to_use: Choosing between different types of databases (e.g., MongoDB vs Cassandra vs SQL)
Designing systems that must stay alive even if the network fails (Partition Tolerance)
Deciding if it's okay for a user to temporarily see old data (Eventual Consistency)
gotchas: Partition Tolerance (P) is NOT optional in real distributed systems (networks always fail eventually), so you are really choosing between C and A during a network split
PACELC is an extension of CAP that also considers Latency vs. Consistency when there is no partition

exercise.prompt: Identify the CAP choice for a specific database behavior.
hints: Available and Partition Tolerant systems don't wait for sync
Consistent and Partition Tolerant systems refuse to answer if they can't sync
exercise.test_code:
assert identify_cap_choice("System remains functional but may return stale data") == "AP"
assert identify_cap_choice("System returns an error if it cannot guarantee the latest data") == "CP"
print("PASS")
